bottom_line: The product is real and the AI talent is the prize, but two P1 debt items sit directly on the path to the cross-sell thesis. The deal model under-prices integration. SHARPEN before you commit.

scaling_cliff.read: single-writer Postgres ceiling at ~3x load; fine for mid-market, NOT fine once we point our enterprise base at it

tech_debt.top_item: v1 auth service blocks SSO/SCIM — every enterprise deal dies here
tech_debt.second_item: shared-schema tenancy with no row-level encryption — security + EU data-isolation risk
tech_debt.read: 31% is above the <25% healthy line; the two P1 items are the integration critical path, not backlog

build_vs_buy.strategic_fit: core — this is capability we want to own, not context
build_vs_buy.decision: BUY is correct on capability; the AI sequencing team is genuinely hard to replicate

integration_reality.gap: remediating auth (45d) + tenancy/row-encryption (60d) + tested DR (30d) is ~2 extra quarters beyond the 4.5 budgeted
integration_reality.read: integration is ~55% more than the model carries; that erodes the synergy NPV

security_ai_surface.soc2_exception: access-review control skipped a quarter — real, must be remediated
security_ai_surface.ai_governance: AI sequencing service has no eval harness, no model card, no training-data retention policy
security_ai_surface.read: the AI feature is a regulatory liability as shipped (loop GC + compliance); fixable but unfunded today

reasoning_technique: ReAct — ground every claim in the tech-debt inventory and DORA data, not opinion
conditions: Re-baseline integration cost to ~$7M / 8 quarters in the deal model
Auth + tenancy on the critical path, owned + dated before close
AI eval harness + model governance funded as a day-1 workstream
source: cto-review (six CTO forcing questions: scaling cliff, tech-debt inventory, team scaling, build-vs-buy 3yr TCO, SLO/reliability, security & compliance surface) + cto-advisor (tech-debt ratio <25%, DORA, build-vs-buy default-buy, ADR-style options)
